new_classes.label_en: Denaturing process (addition of denaturing agents to ethyl alcohol or spirits)
new_classes.label_de: Vergällungsprozess (Zusatz von Vergällungsmitteln zu Ethylalkohol oder Branntwein)
new_classes.definition_en: A process by which ethyl alcohol or other spirits of any alcoholic strength are rendered unfit for human consumption by the addition of denaturing substances, resulting in denatured alcohol classifiable under CN subheading 2207 20.
A substance added to ethyl alcohol or spirits to render them unfit for human consumption, as described in the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2207, fourth paragraph.

new_classes.label_en: Denatured quality (ethyl alcohol or spirits treated with denaturing agents of any strength)
new_classes.label_de: Vergällte Qualität (Ethylalkohol oder Branntwein mit Vergällungsmitteln, bei beliebigem Alkoholgehalt)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by ethyl alcohol or spirits of any alcoholic strength that have been denatured by addition of substances making them unfit for human consumption, distinguishing subheading 2207 20 from undenatured ethyl alcohol of 80% vol or higher under subheading 2207 10.

new_classes.label_en: Denatured ethyl alcohol and other spirits, of any strength (subheading 2207 20)
new_classes.label_de: Vergällter Ethylalkohol und Branntwein mit beliebigem Alkoholgehalt (Unterposition 2207 20)
new_classes.definition_en: Ethyl alcohol and other spirits of any alcoholic strength that have been denatured by addition of substances rendering them unfit for human consumption, classified under CN subheading 2207 20. Covers all alcoholic strengths, distinguishing this subheading from 2207 10 (undenatured ethyl alcohol ≥ 80% vol).

coverage_explanation: The principal distinguishing criteria from the legal text are: (1) the product is ethyl alcohol or other spirits; (2) it has been denatured (of any alcoholic strength). These are captured by the new DenaturedEthylAlcohol22072000 class with the DenaturingQuality restriction, the DenaturingAgentSubstance part restriction, the DenaturingProcess2207 production restriction, and the complement disjointness against UndenaturedEthylAlcohol22071000. The 'any strength' criterion is reflected in the class definition but cannot be expressed as a numeric range restriction because no lower bound applies – this is an open interval covering all ABV values, which is best left as a natural-language annotation. The HS Explanatory Note fourth paragraph cross-reference (specifying which substances qualify as denaturing agents) is noted in the definition but cannot be axiomatised without additional controlled vocabulary for individual denaturing substances (methanol, isopropanol, pyridine, etc.), which are not present in the running TBox. This gap reduces coverage from 1.0 to 0.85.
